name: SQuAD 2.0
description: Stanford Question Answering Dataset with 150K+ reading comprehension Q&A pairs from Wikipedia, including unanswerable questions.
creator: Stanford University

name: GLUE Benchmark
description: General Language Understanding Evaluation: 9 diverse NLP tasks for benchmarking sentence-level language models.
creator: NYU / University of Washington / Google

name: The Pile
description: 886 GB diverse English text corpus from 22 data sources designed for training large language models.
creator: EleutherAI

name: C4 (Colossal Clean Crawled Corpus)
description: Cleaned Common Crawl web text used to pre-train Google's T5 models, filtered for quality.
creator: Google

name: Common Crawl
description: Petabyte-scale raw web crawl archive spanning billions of web pages, used as a foundation for many NLP corpora.
creator: Common Crawl Foundation

name: OpenWebText
description: Open reproduction of OpenAI's WebText: Reddit-linked web pages (3+ upvotes) used to train GPT-2.
creator: Brown University

name: RedPajama-Data-1T
description: Open reproduction of LLaMA's training data with 1T tokens across 7 data sources.
creator: Together AI

name: BookCorpus
description: ~7,185 books scraped from Smashwords, used to pre-train BERT and GPT. Originally reported as 11,000 books; later analysis found ~7,185 unique titles.
creator: University of Toronto

name: WikiText-103
description: High-quality Wikipedia articles for language modeling, with long-range dependency preservation.
creator: Salesforce Research

name: MultiNLI
description: 412K sentence pairs across 10 genres for natural language inference (textual entailment).
creator: NYU

name: ImageNet (ILSVRC)
description: 14M labeled images across ~21,841 synsets (full ImageNet); ILSVRC competition uses 1.2M images across 1,000 categories — the landmark dataset that drove the deep learning revolution.
creator: Princeton University

name: COCO (Common Objects in Context)
description: 330K images with 2.5M object instances annotated for detection, segmentation, and captioning tasks.
creator: Microsoft

name: Re-LAION-5B
description: 5.85 billion image-text pairs filtered from Common Crawl; used to train Stable Diffusion and CLIP. Original LAION-5B was taken down Dec 2023; replaced by Re-LAION-5B (safety-filtered, July 2024).
creator: LAION e.V.

name: Open Images v7
description: 9M images with image-level labels, bounding boxes, segmentation masks, and visual relationships.
creator: Google

name: ADE20K
description: 25K images densely annotated with 150 semantic categories for scene parsing and segmentation.
creator: MIT CSAIL

name: CelebA
description: 202K celebrity face images with 40 binary attribute annotations and 5 facial landmark locations.
creator: MMLAB, The Chinese University of Hong Kong

name: CIFAR-10
description: 60K 32×32 colour images in 10 classes (6K per class), standard image classification benchmark.
creator: University of Toronto

name: LAION-400M
description: 400M English image-text pairs from Common Crawl, predecessor to LAION-5B; used to train early CLIP models.
creator: LAION e.V.

name: LibriSpeech
description: 960-hour English audiobook speech corpus derived from LibriVox, the standard ASR training benchmark.
creator: Johns Hopkins University

name: Common Voice
description: Mozilla's crowd-sourced multilingual speech dataset with 30,000+ validated hours across 100+ languages.
creator: Mozilla Foundation

name: VoxCeleb2
description: 1M+ audio-visual utterances from 6,112 celebrities for speaker recognition and verification.
creator: University of Oxford

name: GigaSpeech
description: 10,000-hour multi-domain English ASR corpus covering audiobooks, podcasts, and YouTube.
creator: Tsinghua University / SpeechColab

name: The Stack
description: Source code in 358 programming languages with permissive licenses, for training large code LLMs.
creator: BigCode / Hugging Face

name: CodeSearchNet
description: 2M (code, documentation) pairs across 6 languages (Python, Java, Go, PHP, Ruby, JavaScript) for code search.
creator: GitHub / Microsoft

name: HumanEval
description: 164 hand-crafted Python programming problems with unit tests to evaluate code generation models.
creator: OpenAI

name: MBPP (Mostly Basic Python Problems)
description: 974 crowd-sourced Python programming problems testing entry-level language understanding.
creator: Google

name: Conceptual Captions (CC3M)
description: 3.3M image-text pairs harvested from the web by filtering and transforming image alt-text.
creator: Google

name: VQA v2
description: 1.1M QA pairs (approximately 443K unique questions with 10 answers each) about 265K COCO images requiring visual and commonsense reasoning.
creator: Virginia Tech / Georgia Tech

name: ShareGPT4V
description: 100K high-quality image-instruction pairs annotated by GPT-4V for visual instruction tuning.
creator: Shanghai AI Laboratory / The Chinese University of Hong Kong (CUHK)

name: Alpaca
description: 52K instruction-following demonstrations generated by GPT-3.5 (text-davinci-003) for fine-tuning LLaMA.
creator: Stanford University

name: FLAN (Fine-tuned LAnguage Net)
description: Instruction-tuning collection: original 2021 FLAN covers 62 NLP tasks; FLAN Collection (2022–2023) expands to 1,836 tasks formatted as natural language instructions.
creator: Google

name: Open Assistant (OASST1)
description: 161K human-generated messages forming 66K conversation trees for RLHF and instruction tuning.
creator: LAION-AI

name: UltraChat
description: 1.5M multi-turn instructional conversations generated by ChatGPT, filtered for quality.
creator: Tsinghua University

name: Dolly 15K
description: 15K high-quality human-generated instruction/response pairs written by Databricks employees.
creator: Databricks

name: MMLU (Massive Multitask Language Understanding)
description: 14,000+ multiple-choice questions spanning 57 academic subjects to test world knowledge and reasoning.
creator: UC Berkeley

name: HellaSwag
description: 70K commonsense NLI examples for sentence completion requiring physical and temporal reasoning.
creator: University of Washington / Allen Institute for AI

name: ARC (AI2 Reasoning Challenge)
description: 7,787 elementary and middle school science exam questions split into Easy and Challenge sets.
creator: Allen Institute for AI

name: TruthfulQA
description: 817 questions designed to detect model hallucinations across 38 categories where humans commonly hold false beliefs.
creator: University of Oxford / OpenAI

name: GSM8K (Grade School Math 8K)
description: 8,500 grade school math word problems requiring multi-step reasoning, created by OpenAI.
creator: OpenAI

name: BIG-bench
description: 204 diverse challenging tasks contributed by 100+ researchers to probe LLM capabilities beyond standard benchmarks.
creator: Google

name: WinoGrande
description: 44K Winograd Schema-style commonsense reasoning problems at scale, minimizing spurious cues.
creator: Allen Institute for AI

name: SuperGLUE
description: Harder successor to GLUE with 8 challenging language understanding tasks requiring more complex reasoning.
creator: NYU / University of Washington / DeepMind / Facebook AI

name: MS MARCO
description: 1M real Bing search queries with 8.8M human-annotated passages for reading comprehension and information retrieval.
creator: Microsoft

name: Natural Questions (NQ)
description: 307K real Google search questions with annotated long and short answers from Wikipedia pages.
creator: Google

name: CoQA
description: 127K questions from 8K conversational reading comprehension passages across 7 domains.
creator: Stanford NLP Group

name: mC4 (Multilingual C4)
description: Multilingual version of C4 covering 101 languages, derived from Common Crawl and used to train mT5.
creator: Google

name: OSCAR
description: Multilingual corpus in 150+ languages extracted and filtered from Common Crawl using language identification.
creator: Inria / Almanach

name: Pile of Law
description: 256 GB legal text corpus from 32 legal data sources for training legal language models.
creator: UC Berkeley / Regents of the University of California

name: SNLI (Stanford Natural Language Inference)
description: 570K human-written English sentence pairs labeled as entailment, contradiction, or neutral; the foundational NLI benchmark.
creator: Stanford NLP Group

name: CNN/DailyMail
description: 311K CNN and Daily Mail news articles paired with bullet-point highlights for abstractive summarization and reading comprehension.
creator: Google DeepMind / Stanford University

name: XSum (Extreme Summarization)
description: 226K BBC news articles each paired with a single-sentence abstract for extreme abstractive summarization.
creator: University of Edinburgh

name: SST-2 (Stanford Sentiment Treebank)
description: 67K movie review sentences with binary positive/negative sentiment labels; one of the core GLUE benchmark tasks.
creator: Stanford University

name: RACE (ReAding Comprehension from Examinations)
description: 97K English reading comprehension questions from Chinese middle and high school exams requiring multi-step reasoning.
creator: Carnegie Mellon University

name: HotpotQA
description: 113K Wikipedia-based multi-hop questions requiring reasoning across two supporting documents to answer.
creator: Carnegie Mellon University / Princeton University

name: FEVER (Fact Extraction and VERification)
description: 185K claims generated from Wikipedia and annotated as Supported, Refuted, or NotEnoughInfo for fact verification.
creator: University of Cambridge / University of Sheffield

name: Dolma
description: 3 trillion token open English corpus from 7 diverse sources (web, books, code, science, etc.) used to train the OLMo LLM.
creator: Allen Institute for AI

name: FineWeb
description: 15T+ token cleaned and deduplicated English web dataset from 96 Common Crawl snapshots (2013–2024) for LLM pretraining.
creator: Hugging Face

name: MNIST
description: 70K grayscale 28×28 handwritten digit images (0–9); the most iconic machine learning benchmark dataset since 1998.
creator: NIST

name: Cityscapes
description: 5K pixel-level semantic segmentation images of urban driving scenes from 50 European cities with 30 semantic categories.
creator: Daimler AG / MPI Informatics / TU Darmstadt

name: FFHQ (Flickr-Faces-HQ)
description: 70K high-quality 1024×1024 face images from Flickr spanning diverse ages, ethnicities, and lighting conditions; used to train StyleGAN.
creator: NVIDIA

name: Places365
description: 1.8M scene-labeled images across 365 place categories from MIT, for training scene recognition models.
creator: MIT CSAIL

name: STL-10
description: 5K labeled training images + 8K test images + 100K unlabeled 96×96 images in 10 classes, designed for unsupervised and semi-supervised feature learning.
creator: Stanford University

name: CIFAR-100
description: 60K 32×32 colour images in 100 fine-grained classes grouped into 20 superclasses; harder variant of CIFAR-10.
creator: University of Toronto

name: ChestX-ray14 (NIH Chest X-rays)
description: 112K frontal chest X-ray images from 30,805 patients annotated with 14 thoracic disease labels for medical image classification.
creator: National Institutes of Health (NIH)

name: TIMIT
description: 6,300 phonetically transcribed sentences from 630 speakers of 8 US English dialects; the foundational acoustic-phonetic corpus.
creator: DARPA / Texas Instruments / MIT

name: LJSpeech
description: 13,100 short audio clips (~24 hours) of a single English speaker reading passages from 7 non-fiction books; the standard TTS benchmark.
creator: LJSpeech

name: SpeechCommands v2
description: 105K one-second recordings of 35 spoken keywords by thousands of speakers for training keyword spotting and wake-word detection models.
creator: Google

name: TED-LIUM 3
description: 452-hour English speech corpus from TED talks with manual transcriptions covering a wide range of speakers and topics.
creator: LIUM, University of Le Mans

name: APPS (Automated Programming Progress Standard)
description: 10K Python programming problems at introductory, interview, and competition levels with test cases for code generation evaluation.
creator: UC Berkeley

name: SWE-bench
description: 2,294 real GitHub issues from 12 Python repositories requiring code changes to resolve, for evaluating software engineering agents.
creator: Princeton NLP Group

name: DS-1000
description: 1,000 Python data science coding problems from Stack Overflow spanning NumPy, Pandas, Matplotlib, TensorFlow, PyTorch, Scipy, and Sklearn.
creator: UCLA / Stanford University

name: CodeContests
description: 13,328 competitive programming problems from Codeforces and other sources with test cases and solutions in multiple languages; used to train AlphaCode.
creator: DeepMind

name: Visual Genome
description: 108K images densely annotated with region descriptions, objects, attributes, and relationships (~1.7M QA pairs) for visual reasoning and scene understanding.
creator: Stanford University

name: Flickr30K
description: 31,783 Flickr images each annotated with 5 crowd-sourced captions; a key benchmark for image captioning and image-text retrieval.
creator: University of Illinois at Urbana-Champaign

name: NoCaps
description: 4,500 Open Images validation images with ~45,000 human-written captions covering novel objects not seen in COCO training data, for zero-shot image captioning evaluation.
creator: Allen Institute for AI / University of Illinois / Cornell University

name: LLaVA-Instruct-150K
description: 158K visual instruction-following samples (conversations, descriptions, reasoning) generated using GPT-4 and COCO images.
creator: University of Wisconsin-Madison

name: ShareGPT
description: ~90K real multi-turn ChatGPT conversations voluntarily shared by users online; widely used for Vicuna and other instruction-tuned models.
creator: ShareGPT

name: LIMA (Less Is More for Alignment)
description: 1,030 carefully hand-selected prompt/response pairs from community forums and expert writing, showing quality beats quantity for alignment.
creator: Meta AI

name: OpenOrca
description: 4.2M GPT-4 (1M) and GPT-3.5 (3.2M) augmented FLAN Collection reasoning chains for training smaller aligned language models.
creator: Open-Orca community

name: WizardLM Evol-Instruct 70K
description: 70K complex instruction-response pairs generated through iterative in-context instruction evolution from simpler seed instructions.
creator: Microsoft Research

name: Alpaca-GPT4
description: 52K Stanford Alpaca instructions regenerated using GPT-4 for significantly higher quality instruction-following demonstrations.
creator: University of Illinois / Microsoft Research

name: Anthropic HH-RLHF
description: 161K human preference comparisons of AI responses rated for helpfulness and harmlessness; the standard RLHF training dataset.
creator: Anthropic

name: UltraFeedback
description: 64K prompts with 256K AI-generated responses scored by GPT-4 across four fine-grained dimensions for reward model training.
creator: Tsinghua University / OpenBMB

name: CommonsenseQA
description: 12,247 multiple-choice questions requiring commonsense knowledge, generated using ConceptNet to ensure challenging distractors.
creator: Tel Aviv University

name: PIQA (Physical Intuition QA)
description: 16,113 multiple-choice questions about physical intuition and everyday activities requiring common-sense physical reasoning.
creator: Allen Institute for AI

name: SocialIQA
description: 38K multiple-choice questions about social situations testing emotional and social intelligence in everyday scenarios.
creator: Allen Institute for AI

name: DROP (Discrete Reasoning Over Paragraphs)
description: 96,567 reading comprehension questions requiring discrete numerical operations (addition, counting, sorting) over Wikipedia paragraphs.
creator: UC Irvine / Allen Institute for AI

name: MT-Bench
description: 80 carefully crafted multi-turn conversational questions across 8 categories for evaluating LLM instruction-following and reasoning quality.
creator: LMSYS / UC Berkeley

name: MATH (Competition Mathematics)
description: 12,500 high-school and competition math problems (AMC, AIME, etc.) with step-by-step LaTeX solutions across 5 difficulty levels.
creator: UC Berkeley

name: MMMU (Massive Multidisciplinary Multimodal Understanding)
description: 11,550 college-level multimodal questions from 183 subfields spanning 30 disciplines including charts, diagrams, and equations.
creator: University of Waterloo / UCSD / CMU

name: FLORES-200
description: 1,012 sentences per language professionally translated into 200 languages from English Wikipedia for machine translation evaluation.
creator: Meta AI

name: OpenWebMath
description: 6.3M high-quality mathematical web documents (14.7B tokens) extracted from Common Crawl for pretraining math-capable LLMs.
creator: University of Toronto / Vector Institute

name: MIMIC-III
description: De-identified EHR data from 46,520 ICU patients at Beth Israel Deaconess Medical Center (2001–2012) including clinical notes and lab results.
creator: MIT Laboratory for Computational Physiology / BIDMC
